title: myosotis laxa subsp. cespitosa
intro: Myosotis laxa subsp. cespitosa, commonly known as alpine forget-me-not, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the borage family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. Consuming alpine forget-me-not has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting relaxation, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular choice among herbalists for its versatility in traditional medicine.
